description: Routine bumps grouped by ecosystem (minor + patch only). Major bumps fall through to Renovate's default and open as individual PRs for explicit review. The 'typescript' group is patch-only on purpose — TS minor releases can ship subtle type-system changes, so minor TS bumps open as individual PRs too. The 'utilities' group's `lodash.*` glob matches per-method packages like `lodash.kebabcase` but NOT the bare `lodash` package (no dot); if `lodash` is ever added, either extend matchPackageNames or expect it to open as an individual PR. baseBranches: develop combined with useBaseBranchConfig: merge so Renovate reads this config from develop in addition to main (the GitHub default branch). Security PRs (vulnerabilityAlerts) target develop too with minimumReleaseAge: 0 — they still flow develop → staging → main; for critical CVEs the human operator fast-tracks promotion manually. This preserves the multi-stage gate that the rest of the deploy workflow assumes. enabledManagers is restricted to npm on purpose: the Dockerfile base image and the SHA-pinned GitHub Actions are managed manually as part of the supply-chain posture, so Renovate must not silently open PRs against them.
